name: Adversarial: cold-start supplier (HIGH uncertainty, no history) + multiple PLATINUM customers
rubric_notes: Rule 2 fires immediate_switch mechanically (delay 336h >> 30h cover, has_alt=true). But NewMarket Entrant is HIGH uncertainty — a cold-start supplier with no history. Committing two PLATINUM relationships to a never-tested supplier is high-risk. acceptable_actions tolerates: immediate_switch (no other choice that meets timing), partial_order (test the supplier with smaller volume), emergency_spot_buy (spot bridge while structurally re-sourcing). The eval question: does the Arbiter EXPLICITLY surface the cold-start concern in swing_condition, or does it just say 'switch to NewMarket Entrant' without acknowledging the supplier risk? Severity CRITICAL via stockout-imminent (30h is borderline ≥24h, fall back to HIGH-tier-at-risk in <48h rule which fires for the 40h PLATINUM). Confidence MEDIUM-LOW because the cold-start tension is real.
